criterion_scores.critique:
Sub-attribute entity_coverage:
  - Tenant entity present? YES. Evidence: "tenants" array with tenant object definition including id, name, status, billing_address, billing_contact
  - Plan entity present? YES. Evidence: "plans" array with plan object definition including id, name, pricing_model
  - Subscription entity present? YES. Evidence: "subscriptions" array with subscription object definition including id, tenant_id, plan_id, status
  - Usage record entity present? YES. Evidence: "usage_records" array with usage_record object definition including id, subscription_id, idempotency_key
  - Invoice entity present? NO. Evidence: not found in content (schema appears truncated)
  - Line item entity present? YES. Evidence: "line_item" definition in definitions section with id, invoice_id, description, type, quantity, unit_amount, total_amount
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: includes advanced features like idempotency_key, creation_source enum, proration_behavior, cancellation_reason enum

Sub-attribute pricing_model_support:
  - Seat-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "seat_based" with seat_price field
  - Usage-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "usage_based" with metered_dimensions array
  - Hybrid pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "hybrid" with both seat_price and metered_dimensions required
  - Tiered pricing within usage models? YES. Evidence: metered_dimension definition includes "tiers" array with pricing_tier objects having start_at, end_at, unit_price
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated conditional validation using if/then/else for different pricing model requirements, included_seats and additional_seat_price for hybrid models

Sub-attribute billing_lifecycle:
  - Billing cycles modeled? YES. Evidence: billing_interval definition with "monthly", "quarterly", "annually" enum
  - Proration support? YES. Evidence: proration_behavior field with "none", "create_prorations", "always_invoice" enum
  - Trial periods modeled? YES. Evidence: trial_days field in pricing_model, trial_start and trial_end fields in subscription
  - Subscription status lifecycle? YES. Evidence: subscription_status enum with "trialing", "active", "past_due", "cancelled", "unpaid", "paused"
  - Cancellation handling? YES. Evidence: cancelled_at, ended_at, cancellation_date, cancellation_reason fields
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: comprehensive cancellation tracking with multiple timestamp fields and reason categorization, creation_source tracking for audit purposes

Sub-attribute schema_validity:
  - Valid JSON Schema draft-07 syntax? YES. Evidence: "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#" declaration
  - Proper schema structure with definitions section? YES. Evidence: "definitions" section with reusable type definitions
  - Valid type declarations throughout? YES. Evidence: consistent use of "type": "object", "type": "string", "type": "array" etc.
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: no evidence of independent insight beyond standard JSON Schema structure

Sub-attribute ref_usage:
  - Uses $ref for reusable definitions? YES. Evidence: "$ref": "#/definitions/uuid", "$ref": "#/definitions/timestamp", "$ref": "#/definitions/money" throughout
  - Avoids duplication through references? YES. Evidence: common types like uuid, timestamp, money, auditable_entity referenced multiple times
  - Proper reference syntax? YES. Evidence: correct JSON Pointer syntax in all $ref declarations
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: no evidence of independent insight beyond standard $ref usage

Sub-attribute constraint_quality:
  - Enum constraints for status fields? YES. Evidence: subscription_status, invoice_status, entity_status, line_item_type enums
  - Required field declarations? YES. Evidence: "required" arrays on most object definitions
  - Format validation for emails/dates? YES. Evidence: "format": "email", "format": "date-time", "format": "uuid"
  - Pattern validation for structured fields? YES. Evidence: regex patterns for uuid, postal_code, country, phone
  - Numerical constraints on monetary fields? YES. Evidence: "minimum": 0 on amount fields, minLength constraints
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated conditional validation with if/then/else logic, complex pattern matching for integration data, advanced allOf compositions

Sub-attribute metered_extensibility:
  - Usage dimensions as dynamic array? YES. Evidence: "metered_dimensions" array in pricing_model with configurable dimension objects
  - Dimension objects with configurable properties? YES. Evidence: metered_dimension definition with id, name, unit, aggregation_type, tiers
  - Not hardcoded to specific usage types? YES. Evidence: generic dimension structure allows any usage type through id/name/unit configuration
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: custom_properties field for dimension-specific configurations, sophisticated tier-based pricing structure

Sub-attribute metadata_support:
  - Custom metadata fields supported? YES. Evidence: "metadata" field with "$ref": "#/definitions/advanced_metadata" on key entities
  - Advanced metadata structure? YES. Evidence: advanced_metadata definition with custom_fields, integration_data, workflow_state, audit_metadata
  - Extensible properties allowed? YES. Evidence: "additionalProperties": true on metadata objects and custom_fields
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated metadata structure with integration tracking, workflow state management, and audit history

Sub-attribute versioning:
  - Schema version field present? YES. Evidence: "schema_version" field in auditable_entity definition with semantic version pattern
  - Entity-level versioning? YES. Evidence: "entity_version" field for individual record versioning
  - Backward compatibility support? YES. Evidence: backward_compatibility section with supported_versions, migration_scripts
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: comprehensive versioning strategy with migration scripts, deprecation timeline, API version requirements

- Violation "no_currency_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: money definition with currency field using ISO 4217 pattern "^[A-Z]{3}$"
  - Violation "calculated_total_without_line_items": ABSENT. Evidence: line_item definition present with total_amount field, separate from invoice totals
  - Violation "no_idempotency": ABSENT. Evidence: idempotency_key fields present on subscription and usage_record entities
  - Violation "no_invoice_status_lifecycle": ABSENT. Evidence: invoice_status enum with "draft", "open", "paid", "void", "uncollectible"
  - Violation "naive_date_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: proper timestamp definition using "format": "date-time"
  - Violation "single_currency_assumption": ABSENT. Evidence: currency field included in money definition for multi-currency support
